slides.lead: Demo presentation of the template. All text is sample (dummy) content and only serves to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. This is where the event title, a one-line subtitle and a footer meta row go. Highlight one word with em for the accent color.

slides.lead: Five roles, one shared platform goal.
slides.notes: Team slide. Instead of photos it uses initials in a colored circle, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. A good fit for the start of a block.

slides.lead: Current platform, pipelines and the biggest bottlenecks.
slides.notes: Divider slide between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: Servers, clusters and managed services spin up in minutes. The value comes from how reliably you run them.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: large heading, bold illustration, short caption. Good for a key point or a chapter.

items: fewer failed deploys after adding automated checks
saved per release on manual steps now automated
uptime sustained across the core services
bullets: Measure impact, not activity: the effect on users is what counts.
Start where you already have telemetry and feel the pain most.
A small pipeline with a clear metric beats a big plan without one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a large number and a label, with supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: The pipeline does not wait for a release night, it ships on every commit.
bullets: Every commit into a versioned, reproducible artifact.
Unit, integration and security gates in one run.
To staging automatically, to prod on approval.
Metrics and logs to catch regressions early.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales on its own.

slides.lead: The same workload, two operating models and two cost curves.
cards: We patch servers by hand. Slow and hard to repeat.
Setup lives in scripts. Better, but still drifts over time.
Infrastructure as code, reviewed and reproducible.
Core systems declarative, edge cases handled manually.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparison or listing variants.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of a modern platform.
cards: Containers and functions sized to the workload.
Kubernetes schedules and heals workloads automatically.
Live dashboards for latency, errors and saturation.
Credentials stored and rotated, never in the repo.
Least-privilege access and scanning at every stage.
Pipelines that take code from commit to production.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: Improvements slow down, it pays to harden the basics.
Traffic and services grow fast, automation has to keep up.
The platform detects and recovers from faults on its own.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: Horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a heading and a small caption.

slides.quote: You do not rise to the level of your ambitions, you fall to the level of your pipelines.
slides.by: Sample quote to showcase the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

slides.lead: Simple, but easy to forget under pressure.
bullets: Every service has the name of a person accountable for it.
You know in advance how you will recognize success.
Every change can be reversed in minutes, not hours.
slides.notes: A plain bullet slide with a strong lead word. Text scales automatically to fill the space.

slides.h1: Reliability is a habit, not a heroic.
slides.lead: A pipeline without an owner and a metric quietly rots.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one large sentence centered on the page. Use it for a key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch to the tools and ship a change to staging live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and a call to action.
